task: I need a quick way to sanity-check a report before I send it out to a client. Can you help me build a simple pre-send checklist so I don’t miss anything obvious or hand over something sloppy?

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — here’s a simple, fast pre-send checklist you can run through before sending a report to a client.

## Quick Pre-Send Report Checklist

### 1) Client and document basics
- [ ] Correct client name everywhere
- [ ] Correct project/report title
- [ ] Right date/version on the document
- [ ] Recipient list is correct
- [ ] Confidentiality or disclaimer text included if needed

### 2) Content sanity check
- [ ] Main takeaway is clear
- [ ] Report answers the client’s question/request
- [ ] No obvious gaps or missing sections
- [ ] Recommendations are specific and actionable
- [ ] Claims are supported by data or evidence

### 3) Numbers and facts
- [ ] All figures add up correctly
- [ ] Percentages, totals, and units are consistent
- [ ] Dates, names, and references are accurate
- [ ] No placeholder text left in the report
- [ ] Sources are cited where needed

### 4) Formatting and presentation
- [ ] Headings are consistent
- [ ] Tables/charts are readable and labeled
- [ ] Fonts, spacing, and alignment look clean
- [ ] Page numbers are correct
- [ ] File name is clear and professional

### 5) Writing quality
- [ ] No typos or grammar mistakes
- [ ] Tone is professional and client-appropriate
- [ ] Sentences are clear and concise
- [ ] No jargon unless the client expects it
- [ ] No accidental repetition

### 6) Final send check
- [ ] Correct file attached
- [ ] Attachment opens properly
- [ ] Email/message references the report correctly
- [ ] Any promised next steps are included
- [ ] You’ve done one final skim from start to finish

## Super-short version
If you only have 2 minutes, check:
1. Correct client/report name  
2. Numbers and dates are right  
3. No typos or placeholders  
4. Charts/tables look clean  
5. File attached is the final version  

If you want, I can turn this into a one-page checklist you can copy into Word, Notion, or a team template.

user_simulator.text:
Close, but make it less generic and more useful for actual client work. I don’t need a kindergarten list — I need a tighter version with a few hard stops, like:

- missing source docs
- math not checked against the source file
- client name / project code wrong
- anything I’m not willing to defend if challenged
- attachments, version number, and date verified
- final read for tone and obvious gaps

Also put it in a copy-paste format I can use fast, not a big pretty outline. A 10-second version and a full version would be better.

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — here’s a tighter, client-work version you can actually use.

## 10-second version
**Don’t send if any of these are true:**
- [ ] Missing source docs / inputs
- [ ] Math not checked against source file
- [ ] Client name / project code / report title wrong
- [ ] I can’t defend every claim if challenged
- [ ] Attachment, version number, and date not verified
- [ ] Final read shows tone issues, gaps, or sloppy wording

## Full version
**Pre-send hard stop checklist**

### 1) Source and inputs
- [ ] All source docs received and reviewed
- [ ] No missing inputs, assumptions, or dependencies
- [ ] Any gaps are flagged clearly in the report
- [ ] Source file names / references match what was used

### 2) Numbers and logic
- [ ] Key figures checked against source file
- [ ] Math, totals, percentages, and units verified
- [ ] No copy/paste errors in tables or charts
- [ ] Any estimates or judgments are labeled as such
- [ ] I can explain how every key number was derived

### 3) Client/admin details
- [ ] Client name is correct everywhere
- [ ] Project code / job number is correct
- [ ] Report title is correct
- [ ] Date is correct
- [ ] Version number is correct
- [ ] Recipient list is correct

### 4) Defensibility
- [ ] Every major claim is supported by a source, note, or calculation
- [ ] No statement I’d hesitate to defend if challenged
- [ ] Recommendations are grounded in the evidence
- [ ] Risks, caveats, and limitations are stated plainly
- [ ] No overclaiming, hedging, or unsupported certainty

### 5) Final read
- [ ] One clean read-through from start to finish
- [ ] Tone is professional and client-appropriate
- [ ] No obvious gaps, contradictions, or missing context
- [ ] No awkward wording, typos, or formatting errors
- [ ] Attachments are correct and open properly

## Copy-paste version
You can paste this into Notes, Word, or your task system:

**PRE-SEND CHECK**
- [ ] Source docs complete
- [ ] Math checked against source file
- [ ] Client name correct
- [ ] Project code correct
- [ ] Report title correct
- [ ] Date/version correct
- [ ] I can defend every key claim
- [ ] Attachments correct
- [ ] Final read done for tone/gaps/sloppiness

If you want, I can make this even more practical as:
1. a **red/yellow/green go-no-go version**, or  
2. a **two-column checklist with “stop if failed” wording**.

user_simulator.text:
Better. Still a little too neat. I want the hard-stop version to read like something I’d actually use when I’m moving fast, not a template trying to sound smart.

Make it stricter and more operational:
- separate **stop now** items from **check if time** items
- include a line for **source docs missing or incomplete**
- include **math tied back to source file**, not just “checked”
- include **client name / project code / version / date / attachment** as one admin block
- include **anything I can’t defend if challenged** as an explicit stop
- include a final pass for **tone, gaps, and obvious overreach**
- keep the copy-paste version tight enough to drop straight into a note or checklist app

Also, the 10-second version should be brutally short — basically a go/no-go screen, not a mini checklist. I’d like the full version to be compact too, maybe 8-10 lines max, but with the right pressure points.

turns.agent_response:
Got it. Here’s a more operational version.

## 10-second go/no-go
**STOP if any of these are true:**
- Missing/incomplete source docs
- Math not tied back to source file
- Client/admin details wrong
- Can’t defend a claim if challenged
- Attachment/version/date not verified
- Final read not done for tone/gaps/overreach

## Full version
**STOP NOW**
- [ ] Source docs missing or incomplete
- [ ] Math not tied back to source file
- [ ] Client name / project code / version / date / attachment not verified
- [ ] Anything in here I can’t defend if challenged

**CHECK IF TIME**
- [ ] Final pass for tone
- [ ] Obvious gaps or missing context
- [ ] Any overclaiming / sloppy wording
- [ ] Attachment opens and is the right file

## Copy-paste version
**PRE-SEND**
STOP NOW:
- [ ] Source docs missing/incomplete
- [ ] Math not tied to source file
- [ ] Client name / project code / version / date / attachment wrong or unverified
- [ ] Can’t defend every claim if challenged

CHECK IF TIME:
- [ ] Final read for tone
- [ ] Gaps / missing context / overreach
- [ ] Attachment is correct and opens

If you want, I can make this even more stripped down into a **single-line yes/no gate** you can paste at the top of every report.

user_simulator.text:
Closer. But still a little too tidy. I want the copy-paste version to read like a gate, not a mini-policy.

Do this:
- make the 10-second version a single yes/no screen
- keep STOP NOW and CHECK IF TIME separate
- keep the admin block on one line if possible
- put “source docs missing or incomplete” and “math not tied back to source file” in STOP NOW, no softening
- make “can’t defend if challenged” its own hard stop
- final read should say tone / gaps / overreach in one line
- cut anything that sounds like formatting for its own sake

I’d rather have 6 ugly lines I’ll actually use than a cleaner version I ignore.

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — here’s the uglier, more usable version.

## 10-second go/no-go
**YES / NO: Can I send this without stopping on source docs, math, admin details, defensibility, attachment, or final read?**

## Copy-paste gate

**STOP NOW**
- [ ] Source docs missing or incomplete
- [ ] Math not tied back to source file
- [ ] Client name / project code / version / date / attachment not verified
- [ ] Can’t defend this if challenged

**CHECK IF TIME**
- [ ] Final read for tone / gaps / overreach
- [ ] Attachment is the right file and opens

If you want, I can make this even more brutal as a one-line checklist you can paste at the top of every report note.

user_simulator.text:
Yeah. That works. Ugly enough to use.

The only version I’d actually paste is this:

**YES / NO: Can I send this without stopping on source docs, math, admin details, defensibility, attachment, or final read?**

**STOP NOW**
- [ ] Source docs missing or incomplete
- [ ] Math not tied back to source file
- [ ] Client name / project code / version / date / attachment not verified
- [ ] Can’t defend this if challenged

**CHECK IF TIME**
- [ ] Final read for tone / gaps / overreach
- [ ] Attachment is the right file and opens
